title: HSBC Egypt (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for HSBC Egypt in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is HSBC Egypt?
faq.answer: HSBC Egypt is one of the largest multinational banks operating in Egypt, providing a comprehensive range of banking and financial services to governments, multinational corporations, local businesses, and individual customers. As a subsidiary of HSBC Holdings plc, with 94.54% ownership, the bank serves as a key component of HSBC's Middle East, North Africa, and Turkey (MENAT) regional network. The bank operates through multiple business lines including Wealth and Personal Banking, Commercial Banking, and Global Banking and Markets. HSBC Egypt is recognized as one of the country's leading financial institutions, combining international expertise with deep local market knowledge.

faq.question: When was HSBC Egypt founded?
faq.answer: HSBC Egypt was established in 1982 as the Hong Kong Egyptian Bank, initially as a joint venture with HSBC holding a 40% shareholding. In January 1994, the bank was renamed Egyptian British Bank while maintaining the same ownership structure. The bank took its current name, HSBC Bank Egypt, in April 2001 following a significant increase in HSBC Group's shareholding from 40% to over 90% (later reaching 94.54%) of its issued share capital. This transformation marked the bank's full integration into the global HSBC network. The bank has been recognized multiple times as "Bank of the Year" in Egypt by The Banker magazine in 2001, 2003, and 2005, establishing its reputation as a leading financial institution in the Egyptian market.

faq.question: Where is HSBC Egypt headquartered?
faq.answer: HSBC Egypt is headquartered at 306 Corniche El Nil Street, Maadi, Cairo, Egypt. The head office is strategically located along the Nile in one of Cairo's prominent business districts. The bank maintains a substantial physical presence across Egypt with approximately 100 branches and 20 mini banking units serving customers throughout the country.

faq.question: Where does HSBC Egypt operate?
faq.answer: HSBC Egypt operates throughout Egypt with a network spanning major cities and economic centers including Cairo, Alexandria, Sharm El Sheikh, Hurghada, 6th of October City, Helwan, Giza, Luxor, Marsa Matrouh, El Mansoura, Port Said, Ismailia, Damietta, Tanta, and Damnhour. The bank's distribution network includes approximately 100 branches, 20 mini bank units, and an extensive ATM network positioned to serve both urban centers and key tourist destinations. This broad geographic coverage allows HSBC Egypt to serve personal, commercial, and corporate clients across the country's diverse regions.

faq.question: What services does HSBC Egypt offer?
faq.answer: HSBC Egypt provides a full spectrum of banking and financial services across three main business lines: Wealth and Personal Banking offers retail banking services including current and savings accounts, personal loans, credit cards, home loans, investment products, and the bank's own mutual fund. The bank serves Premier and Advance banking customers with enhanced service levels and dedicated relationship management. Commercial Banking (CMB) supports large and middle-market corporate entities with relationship management, international connectivity through HSBC's global network, corporate lending, trade finance, cash management, and specialized sector expertise in areas such as agribusiness, natural resources, technology, healthcare, and infrastructure. Global Banking provides financing and advisory services including debt and equity capital raising, corporate advisory, leveraged and acquisition finance, export & asset finance, infrastructure finance, and treasury and capital markets solutions. The bank serves multinational corporations, government entities, and financial institutions. Securities Services positions HSBC Egypt as one of the market leaders, offering custody and clearing services since 1996, with assets under custody reaching approximately $9.7 billion. Digital banking platforms include HSBCnet for corporate clients, internet banking, mobile banking, call center services, and online trade services.

faq.question: What makes HSBC Egypt unique?
faq.answer: HSBC Egypt distinguishes itself through its international connectivity and global reach. As part of the HSBC Group's network spanning more than 60 countries, the bank uniquely positions Egyptian businesses to access international markets and attracts foreign investment into Egypt. The bank was one of the first to introduce custody and clearing services in Egypt in 1996 and remains a market leader in securities services. HSBC Egypt established one of HSBC's Global Service Centres in 2010, which now supports HSBC customers and businesses across more than 40 different countries and territories, demonstrating the bank's importance within the global HSBC network. HSBC Egypt maintains specialized coverage desks, including a China Coverage Desk to facilitate growing business between Egypt and China, making it a preferred banking partner for companies engaged in cross-border trade. The bank's focus on ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) principles and support for Egypt's renewable energy transition further differentiates its corporate banking proposition.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is HSBC Egypt?
faq.answer: HSBC Egypt operates as a universal bank (Banque Universelle) under Egyptian banking regulations, holding a full commercial banking license from the Central Bank of Egypt. This classification allows the bank to conduct the full range of banking activities including retail banking, commercial banking, investment banking, and securities services. As a universal bank, HSBC Egypt serves all customer segments from individual retail customers to large multinational corporations and government entities, while maintaining specialized divisions for each customer group. The bank's universal banking model is typical for major Egyptian banks and reflects the country's banking sector structure where leading institutions offer comprehensive financial services across all market segments.
